html:
[1]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 少落魄，不事家产 - Describes someone who is dissolute and neglects family affairs.
  • 心气闲纵 - Refers to a relaxed and unrestrained temperament.
  • 嗜酒邪游 - Indicates a fondness for alcohol and wandering in a morally questionable manner.
  • 投于亲故 - Refers to seeking help from relatives and friends.
  • 见弃 - Means to be abandoned or rejected.
  • 饥寒之色可掬 - A vivid description of extreme hunger and cold, suggesting a pitiful appearance.
  • 仰天长吁 - Describes looking up to the sky and sighing deeply.
  • 几缗则丰用 - Asks how much money would be sufficient for a comfortable life.
  • 三五万则可以活矣 - States that 30,000 to 50,000 coins would be enough to survive.
  • 十万 - Refers to 100,000 coins.
  • 百万 - Refers to 1,000,000 coins.
  • 三百万 - Refers to 3,000,000 coins.
  • 袖出一缗 - Describes taking out a string of coins from one's sleeve.
  • 给子今夕 - Means to provide for the person for the evening.
  • 明日午时俟子于西市波斯邸 - Instructs the person to wait at a Persian inn in the West Market the next day at noon.
  • 慎无后期 - Warns against being late.
  • 不告姓名而去 - Indicates that the elder did not reveal his name before leaving.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 《玄怪录》 - The title of the collection, which records strange and supernatural events.
  • 唐 牛僧孺 - The author, a prominent figure during the Tang Dynasty.
  • 周、隋间人 - Refers to someone living during the transition between the Zhou and Sui dynasties, indicating a long lifespan or a legendary status.
  • 长安 - The capital city of the Tang Dynasty, known for its grandeur and cultural significance.
  • 东市西门 - A specific location in Chang'an, the West Gate of the East Market.
  • 波斯邸 - A Persian inn, reflecting the cosmopolitan nature of Chang'an with its diverse foreign communities.

Proper Nouns

  • 《玄怪录》 - The title of the collection.
  • 牛僧孺 - The author of the collection.
  • 杜子春 - The protagonist of the story.
  • 长安 - The capital city of the Tang Dynasty.
  • 东市西门 - A specific location in Chang'an.
  • 波斯邸 - A Persian inn in Chang'an.

[2]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 子春既富，荡心复炽 - 子春 became wealthy, but his desire to spend extravagantly flared up again. '荡心' (reckless heart) and '炽' (flaring up) describe a sudden and intense desire for luxury.
  • 乘肥衣轻 - Riding in luxurious carriages and wearing fine clothes, a common idiom in Classical Chinese to describe living a life of luxury.
  • 会酒徒，徵丝竹歌舞于倡楼 - Gathered with drinking companions, he hired musicians and dancers from brothels. '倡楼' refers to brothels or entertainment venues.
  • 治生 - Making a living or earning a livelihood, a term often used in Classical Chinese to describe the pursuit of wealth or business.
  • 倏忽如初 - Suddenly, he was back to his original state. '倏忽' means 'suddenly' or 'in a flash.'
  • 自叹于市门 - Lamented by the city gate, a phrase that suggests a public or visible expression of regret or frustration.
  • 握其手曰 - Grasped his hand and said, a formal way of introducing direct speech.
  • 君复如此，奇哉 - You are back to this, how extraordinary. '奇哉' expresses surprise or astonishment.
  • 几缗方可 - How many strings of coins are enough? '缗' refers to a unit of currency (1000 coins).
  • 愧谢而已 - Only apologized in embarrassment, a phrase that conveys a sense of shame and regret.
  • 明日午时，来前期处 - Come to the same place tomorrow at noon. '前期处' refers to the previous meeting place.
  • 得钱一千万 - Received one million coins, where '一千万' is a large sum of money.
  • 石季伦、猗顿小竖耳 - Stone Jilun and Yi Dun are just insignificant figures. '小竖' is a derogatory term for insignificant people.
  • 纵适之情，又却如故 - His desire for pleasure returned to its former state. '纵适' means 'indulging in pleasure.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 子春 - A fictional or historical figure, no specific historical figure is referenced here.
  • 石季伦 - Shi Chong (石崇), a wealthy and extravagant official during the Western Jin Dynasty.
  • 猗顿 - Yi Dun (猗頓), a famous merchant during the Warring States period known for his wealth.
  • 倡楼 - Refers to brothels or entertainment venues, where musicians and dancers were hired for entertainment.

Proper Nouns

  • 子春 - A fictional or historical figure, no specific translation required.
  • 石季伦 - Shi Chong (石崇), a wealthy and extravagant official during the Western Jin Dynasty.
  • 猗顿 - Yi Dun (猗頓), a famous merchant during the Warring States period known for his wealth.
  • 倡楼 - No specific translation required, as it refers to a general type of entertainment venue.

[3]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 复遇老人于故处 - Encountered the old man again at the same place; describes a recurring encounter.
  • 不胜其愧 - Unable to bear the shame; conveys a deep sense of guilt or embarrassment.
  • 掩面而走 - Covered his face and ran away; describes a person feeling so ashamed that they hide their face and flee.
  • 牵裾止之 - Pulled his sleeve to stop him; a polite and respectful way to detain someone.
  • 嗟乎！拙谋也 - Alas! It was a poor plan; expresses regret and acknowledges a mistake.
  • 因与三千万 - Then gave him three million; describes a significant financial transaction.
  • 此而不痊 - If this does not heal; indicates a potential outcome.
  • 则子贫在膏肓矣 - Then you will be poor beyond help; suggests that the situation is hopeless or irreparable.
  • 吾落魄邪游 - I am a wandering, disreputable person; reflects a state of being aimless and without a fixed social position.
  • 生涯罄尽 - My life's resources are exhausted; indicates that all resources have been used up.
  • 亲戚豪族，无相顾者 - Neither relatives nor wealthy families care for me; highlights social isolation.
  • 我何以当之 - How can I repay such kindness?; a rhetorical question expressing gratitude and a sense of duty.
  • 感叟深惠 - Grateful for the old man's deep kindness; conveys deep appreciation.
  • 立事之后 - After establishing myself; refers to achieving success or stability.
  • 唯叟所使 - At the old man's command; indicates a willingness to serve or follow the old man's instructions.
  • 吾心也 - It is my heart; conveys sincerity and genuine intention.
  • 子治生毕 - When you have finished managing your affairs; refers to completing one's business or responsibilities.
  • 来岁中元 - Next year during the Zhongyuan Festival; specifies a particular time in the lunar calendar.
  • 见我于老君双桧下 - Meet me under the two cypress trees of Laojun; specifies a location for the meeting.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 中元 - The Zhongyuan Festival, also known as the Ghost Festival, is a traditional Chinese festival held on the 15th day of the seventh month of the lunar calendar. It is a time when the spirits of the dead are believed to return to the world of the living.
  • 老君 - Laojun, or Laozi, is a revered figure in Taoism, often associated with the founder of Taoism and the author of the *Tao Te Ching*.
  • 双桧 - Two cypress trees, which are often associated with longevity and immortality in Chinese culture. Cypress trees are also commonly found near temples and are considered sacred.

Proper Nouns

  • 中元 - The Zhongyuan Festival.
  • 老君 - Laojun, or Laozi, a revered figure in Taoism.
  • 双桧 - Two cypress trees, often associated with longevity and immortality.

[4]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 方啸 - A poetic way of saying 'was singing' or 'was chanting.'
  • 遂与 - Indicates a sequence of actions.
  • 入 - Means 'to enter' or 'to proceed into.'
  • 严洁 - Emphasizes cleanliness and orderliness.
  • 遥覆 - Suggests a distant and expansive coverage.
  • 鸾鹤 - Refers to mythical birds often associated with immortals.
  • 紫焰 - Describes a purple flame.
  • 灼焕 - Means 'to shine brightly.'
  • 环炉而立 - Describes the arrangement of the jade maidens.
  • 分据 - Means 'to stand in positions.'
  • 慎勿 - A strong admonition.
  • 虽 - Introduces a concessive clause.
  • 及 - Means 'even if' or 'and.'
  • 万苦 - Means 'all kinds of suffering.'
  • 但当 - Means 'just' or 'only.'
  • 安心 - Means 'be at peace.'
  • 终 - Means 'ultimately' or 'finally.'
  • 一心念 - Means 'to concentrate on.'
  • 言讫 - Means 'after speaking.'
  • 而已 - Means 'only' or 'nothing more.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 老人者 - Refers to an old man, possibly an immortal or a wise figure.
  • 二桧之阴 - Refers to the shade of two cypress trees, which are often symbolic of longevity and wisdom in Chinese culture.
  • 华山云台峰 - Refers to the Yuntai Peak of Mount Hua, a sacred mountain in Chinese mythology.
  • 玉女 - Refers to jade maidens, often associated with immortals or deities.
  • 青龙白虎 - Refers to the green dragon and white tiger, which are mythological creatures often associated with the four cardinal directions and the elements of water and metal.
  • 夜叉 - Refers to yakshas, mythological beings often depicted as fierce and fearsome.
  • 猛兽 - Refers to fierce beasts, often symbolic of danger and chaos.
  • 地狱 - Refers to hell, a place of punishment in Chinese Buddhist and Taoist cosmology.
  • 亲属 - Refers to relatives, emphasizing the personal and familial aspects of the experience.

Proper Nouns

  • 华山云台峰 - The Yuntai Peak of Mount Hua.
  • 玉女 - Jade maidens.
  • 青龙白虎 - Green dragon and white tiger.
  • 夜叉 - Yakshas.
  • 地狱 - Hell.

[5]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 道士適去 - The Taoist just left, indicating a recent departure.
  • 旌旗戈甲，千乘万骑 - Flags, spears, and armor, a thousand chariots and ten thousand horses, a vivid description of a grand military procession.
  • 遍满崖谷来 - Filling the ravines and valleys, emphasizing the vastness and intensity of the scene.
  • 呵叱之声动天 - The shouts and commands shook the heavens, a hyperbolic expression of the loudness and authority of the commands.
  • 身长丈余 - Over ten feet tall, a superlative description of the general's height.
  • 人马皆着金甲 - Both men and horses were clad in golden armor, highlighting the grandeur and wealth of the procession.
  • 光芒射人 - Shining brightly, describing the reflective quality of the golden armor.
  • 亲卫数百人 - Hundreds of personal guards, indicating a large and loyal entourage.
  • 拔剑张弓 - Drawing swords and stringing bows, a gesture of readiness and aggression.
  • 直入堂前 - Straight into the hall, emphasizing the direct and forceful entry.
  • 汝是何人，敢不避大将军 - Who are you, and dare you not avoid the Great General, a confrontational question.
  • 左右竦剑而前 - The attendants drew their swords and advanced, indicating a threatening posture.
  • 逼问姓名 - Forced to ask for names, suggesting a coercive interrogation.
  • 作何物 - What are you doing, a question about the individual's actions.
  • 皆不对 - All answered not, indicating a refusal to respond.
  • 问者大怒，催斩，争射之 - The questioner grew furious, urging execution, and shooting arrows, a description of escalating violence.
  • 声如雷 - The sound was like thunder, emphasizing the intensity of the attack.
  • 竟不应 - Yet still no response, indicating a continued silence.
  • 拗怒而去 - Left in anger, describing the departure of the general.
  • 猛虎、毒龙、狻猊、狮子、腹蛇万计 - Thousands of tigers, venomous dragons, lion-like beasts, lions, and vipers, a list of dangerous animals.
  • 哮吼拿攫而争前 - Roaring, snarling, and clawing, they rushed forward, describing the aggressive behavior of the animals.
  • 欲搏噬 - Wishing to seize and devour, indicating the animals' intent to attack.
  • 或跳过其上 - Or leaping over them, describing the animals' movements.
  • 子春神色不动 - Zi Chun remained unperturbed, indicating calmness in the face of danger.
  • 有顷而散 - After a while, they dispersed, describing the end of the disturbance.
  • 大雨滂澍 - Heavy rain poured down, a vivid description of the weather.
  • 雷电晦暝 - Thunder and lightning darkened the sky, emphasizing the storm's intensity.
  • 火轮走其左右 - Fire wheels moved around them, a metaphorical description of the storm's effects.
  • 电光掣其前后 - Lightning flashed before and behind them, adding to the dramatic imagery.
  • 目不得开 - Unable to open their eyes, indicating the blinding effect of the storm.
  • 庭际水深丈余 - The water in the courtyard was over ten feet deep, emphasizing the severity of the flooding.
  • 流电吼雷 - Flowing lightning and roaring thunder, a vivid description of the storm.
  • 势若山川开破，不可制止 - The force was like mountains and rivers being torn apart, unstoppable, a hyperbolic description of the storm's power.
  • 瞬息之间，波及坐下 - In an instant, the waves reached under their feet, indicating the rapid spread of the flood.
  • 端坐不顾 - Sitting calmly without looking up, describing Zi Chun's composure.
  • 未顷而散 - After a short while, it dispersed, indicating the end of the storm.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 道士 - Refers to a Taoist priest or practitioner, indicating a spiritual or mystical context.
  • 大将军 - A high-ranking military title, suggesting a powerful and authoritative figure.
  • 金甲 - Golden armor, symbolizing wealth, power, and military might.
  • 亲卫 - Personal guards, indicating a close and loyal entourage.
  • 猛虎、毒龙、狻猊、狮子、腹蛇 - These are mythological and real animals, often used in Chinese literature to represent danger and chaos.
  • 大雨滂澍 - Heavy rain, often associated with natural disasters or divine intervention.
  • 雷电晦暝 - Thunder and lightning, often used to create a dramatic and ominous atmosphere.
  • 火轮 - Fire wheels, a metaphorical description of the storm's effects.
  • 电光 - Lightning, a common element in Chinese literature to create a dramatic and intense scene.
  • 庭际水深丈余 - Deep water in the courtyard, indicating a severe natural disaster.
  • 流电吼雷 - Flowing lightning and roaring thunder, a vivid description of a powerful storm.
  • 势若山川开破，不可制止 - The force of the storm is compared to the tearing apart of mountains and rivers, emphasizing its unstoppable nature.

Proper Nouns

  • 子春 - The name of the subject, likely a historical or literary figure.

[6]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 引牛头狱卒 - Vivid description of a terrifying executioner, literally 'dragging a head of a bull as a jailer.'
  • 奇貌鬼神 - Describes the executioner's appearance as bizarre and supernatural.
  • 大镬汤 - Refers to a large cauldron of boiling water used for punishment.
  • 将大镬汤而置子春前 - Dramatic action of placing the cauldron in front of Zi Chun.
  • 长枪刃叉 - Refers to long spears and forks used as instruments of torture.
  • 四面周匝 - Means 'all around' or 'surrounding.'
  • 肯言姓名即放 - Means 'if you say your name, you will be released.'
  • 不肯言，即当心叉取置之镬中 - Means 'if you don't speak, we will impale you and throw you into the cauldron.'
  • 捽于阶下 - Means 'dragged down the steps.'
  • 指曰 - Means 'pointing and saying.'
  • 言姓名免之 - Means 'say your name to save yourself.'
  • 鞭捶流血 - Describes the use of whips and beatings causing bloodshed.
  • 或射或斫 - Means 'or shooting or chopping.'
  • 或煮或烧 - Means 'or boiling or burning.'
  • 苦不可忍 - Means 'the suffering is unbearable.'
  • 号哭 - Means 'weeping and crying.'
  • 诚为陋拙 - Means 'truly, I am unworthy and clumsy.'
  • 有辱君子 - Means 'a disgrace to a gentleman.'
  • 幸得执巾栉 - Means 'fortunately, I have served you for many years.'
  • 奉事十余年矣 - Means 'serving you for over ten years.'
  • 今为尊鬼所执 - Means 'now I am held by a powerful spirit.'
  • 不胜其苦 - Means 'unable to bear the suffering.'
  • 不敢望君匍匐拜乞 - Means 'I dare not hope that you will crawl and beg.'
  • 望君一言 - Means 'hope for a word from you.'
  • 即全性命矣 - Means 'to save my life.'
  • 人谁无情 - Means 'who among people has no feelings?'
  • 君乃忍惜一言 - Means 'you are willing to spare just one word.'
  • 雨泪庭中 - Means 'weeping tears in the courtyard.'
  • 且咒且骂 - Means 'cursing and scolding.'
  • 子春终不顾 - Means 'Zi Chun remained unmoved.'
  • 吾不能毒汝妻耶 - Means 'I cannot harm your wife?'
  • 令取锉碓 - Means 'ordered to fetch a millstone.'
  • 从脚寸寸坐刂之 - Means 'cutting her from the feet upwards.'
  • 叫哭愈急 - Means 'crying more desperately.'
  • 此贼妖术已成 - Means 'this demon's magic is complete.'
  • 不可使久在世间 - Means 'cannot allow him to stay in the world for long.'
  • 敕左右斩之 - Means 'ordered the attendants to behead him.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 将军 - Refers to a high-ranking military officer.
  • 狱卒 - Refers to prison guards or executioners.
  • 镬汤 - A large cauldron of boiling water used for punishment.
  • 长枪刃叉 - Traditional weapons used in executions and torture.
  • 鞭捶 - Traditional methods of corporal punishment.
  • 射或斫 - Methods of execution involving shooting or chopping.
  • 煮或烧 - Methods of execution involving boiling or burning.
  • 锉碓 - A tool used for grinding or crushing.
  • 妖术 - Refers to magic or sorcery.
  • 斩之 - Refers to beheading as a method of execution.

Proper Nouns

  • 子春 - The name of the person being tortured.
  • 将军 - A high-ranking military officer.
  • 狱卒 - Prison guards or executioners.
  • 尊鬼 - A powerful spirit or demon.
  • 锉碓 - A tool used for grinding or crushing.

[7]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 魂魄被领见阎罗王 - Literally means 'the soul and spirit are led to see the King of Hell.' Common idiom in Chinese literature to describe the journey of a deceased person to the afterlife.
  • 此乃云台峰妖民乎 - Rhetorical question asking if the person is an 'evil person from Yuntai Peak.' '妖民' refers to a person with supernatural or malevolent qualities.
  • 熔铜、铁杖、碓捣、硙磨、火坑、镬汤、刀山、剑林之苦 - List of tortures in hell, including molten copper, iron rods, pestles and mortars, fire pits, boiling cauldrons, knife mountains, and sword forests. Symbolic representations of severe punishments in Buddhist and Taoist cosmology.
  • 亦似可忍 - Means 'it seems bearable' or 'it can be endured.'
  • 竟不呻吟 - Means 'he/she never groaned' or 'he/she remained silent.'
  • 阴贼 - Refers to someone who is evil or treacherous.
  • 宜令作女人 - Means 'it is appropriate to make him/her a woman.'
  • 配生宋州单父县丞王勤家 - Means 'destined to be reborn in the family of Wang Qin, the county magistrate of Shanfu, Song Prefecture.'
  • 容色绝代 - Means 'beauty unparalleled' or 'unmatched beauty.'
  • 亲戚相狎，侮之万端 - Means 'relatives and friends mock and tease her in countless ways.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 阎罗王 - The King of Hell, a figure in Chinese Buddhism and Taoism who judges the souls of the dead.
  • 云台峰 - A mountain peak, often associated with supernatural or mystical significance in Chinese literature.
  • 妖民 - A person with supernatural or evil qualities, often used in stories of the supernatural.
  • 熔铜、铁杖、碓捣、硙磨、火坑、镬汤、刀山、剑林 - Specific tortures in hell, each representing a different form of punishment. Symbolic and not literal descriptions of physical torture.
  • 宋州单父县丞王勤家 - Refers to a specific family in a specific location, indicating the rebirth of the soul in a particular context.
  • 亲戚相狎，侮之万端 - Reflects the social dynamics and relationships within a family, where the reborn soul is subjected to ridicule and mistreatment.

Proper Nouns

  • 阎罗王 - The King of Hell.
  • 云台峰 - Yuntai Peak.
  • 宋州单父县丞王勤家 - The family of Wang Qin, the county magistrate of Shanfu, Song Prefecture.

[8]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 聞者容而慕之 - Hearing of her beauty and virtue, he admired her. Combines admiration for both physical and moral qualities.
  • 因媒氏求焉 - Through a matchmaker, he sought her hand. Refers to the traditional practice of using a matchmaker to arrange marriages.
  • 其家以哑辞之 - Her family declined because she was mute. Indicates a specific reason for the initial refusal.
  • 苟为妻而贤，何用言矣 - If she is a good wife, why does it matter if she cannot speak? Rhetorical question emphasizing the value of character over speech.
  • 亦足以戒长舌之妇 - It is also sufficient to warn against the gossipy women. Suggests that the story serves as a cautionary tale.
  • 生一男，仅二岁，聪慧无敌 - She bore a son, who was only two years old but incredibly intelligent. Highlights the exceptional nature of the child.
  • 昔贾大夫之妻鄙其夫才不笑尔 - In the past, the wife of Duke Jia despised her husband because he lacked wit. Refers to a historical anecdote about a wife who looked down on her husband's lack of intelligence.
  • 然观其射雉，尚释其憾 - However, when she saw him shoot pheasants, she still let go of her resentment. Shows a moment of reconciliation.
  • 今吾陋不及贾，而文艺非徒射雉也 - Now, I am inferior to Duke Jia, and my literary and artistic talents are not just for shooting pheasants. Expresses the speaker's self-deprecation and comparison.
  • 大丈夫为妻所鄙，安用其子 - A man who is despised by his wife, what use is his son? Rhetorical question underscoring the speaker's despair.
  • 乃持两足，以头扑于石上，应手而卒 - He then grabbed the child's feet and struck his head against a rock, killing him instantly. Describes a violent act.
  • 子春爱生于心，忽忘其约，不觉失声云 - Zi Chun, filled with love for the child, forgot his vow and unconsciously exclaimed. Shows a moment of emotional conflict.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 卢珪 - A name, likely a fictional character or a historical figure.
  • 贾大夫 - Refers to Duke Jia, a historical figure known for his wife's disdain for his lack of wit.
  • 子春 - A name, likely a fictional character or a historical figure.
  • 长舌之妇 - Refers to gossipy women, a common theme in classical Chinese literature.
  • 射雉 - Refers to the act of shooting pheasants, often used as a metaphor for hunting or skill.

Proper Nouns

  • 卢珪 - A name, likely a fictional character or a historical figure.
  • 贾大夫 - A historical figure.
  • 子春 - A name, likely a fictional character or a historical figure.

[9]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 噫 - An interjection expressing surprise, disappointment, or exclamation, often translated as 'Ah!' or 'Alas!'
  • 措大 - A term for a scholar or student, often used in a derogatory sense.
  • 紫焰穿屋上天 - A vivid description of a purple flame shooting through the house and into the sky, possibly symbolizing supernatural or divine intervention.
  • 火起四舍 - Means 'fire broke out in all four quarters,' indicating a widespread fire.
  • 屋室俱焚 - Means 'all houses and rooms were burned down.'
  • 吾子之心，喜怒哀惧恶欲，皆能忘也 - A statement about the person's heart being free from the five emotions (joy, anger, sorrow, fear, desire).
  • 所未臻者，爱而已 - Means 'the only thing that has not been achieved is love.'
  • 向使子无‘噫’声 - Means 'if you had not made that ‘Ah!’ sound.'
  • 吾之药成，子亦上仙矣 - Means 'my medicine would have been successful, and you would have become an immortal.'
  • 嗟乎，仙才之难得也 - Means 'Alas, it is difficult to find someone with the talent to become an immortal.'
  • 吾药可重炼，而子之身犹为世界所容矣 - Means 'my medicine can be refined again, and your body is still part of the world.'
  • 勉之哉 - Means 'Be diligent!' or 'Strive!'
  • 遥指路使归 - Means 'pointed to the road to send you back.'
  • 子春强登基观焉 - Means 'Zi Chun forced his way up to look.'
  • 炉已坏 - Means 'the furnace was already ruined.'
  • 中有铁柱大如臂，长数尺 - Means 'there was an iron pillar as thick as an arm and several feet long.'
  • 道士脱衣，以刀子削之 - Means 'the Taoist priest took off his clothes and used a knife to shave it.'
  • 愧其忘誓 - Means 'feeling ashamed for breaking his vow.'

  • 赵 - Likely a proper name, possibly referring to Zhao, a common Chinese surname.
  • 裴 - Likely a proper name, possibly referring to Pei, another common Chinese surname.
  • 黄头 - Refers to a servant or attendant, often depicted as a low-ranking individual.
  • 九天画堂 - Refers to a grand and divine hall, often used in mythological or religious contexts to denote a place of great significance.
  • 方外之交 - Implies a spiritual or philosophical connection, often associated with Daoist or Buddhist traditions.
  • 云韶九奏之乐 - Refers to the highest and most refined music, often associated with the celestial realm or divine music.
  • 生死海 - A metaphor for the cycle of birth and death, often used in Buddhist contexts.
  • 宿命 - Refers to predestined fate or karma, suggesting that Zhao's presence at the gathering is part of a larger, predetermined plan.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 裴谓敬伯曰 - A formal way of introducing direct speech, meaning 'Pei told Jingbo.'
  • 评公使车留此一宿 - Literal meaning 'the envoy's carriage staying here for one night.' '评公使车' refers to the official carriage used by an envoy.
  • 得无惊群将乎 - A rhetorical question meaning 'would it not disturb the troops?' '得无' is an archaic way of expressing doubt or a rhetorical question.
  • 宜且就馆 - Means 'it is best to temporarily stay in the guesthouse.' '宜' suggests advisability, and '就馆' means to stay in a guesthouse.
  • 未赴阙闲时 - Means 'before the time of reporting to the imperial court.' '阙' refers to the imperial court or the emperor's palace.
  • 访我可也 - Means 'you may visit me.' '访' means to visit, and '可也' is an archaic way of saying 'you may.'
  • 尘路遐远 - Literally means 'dusty road far away,' referring to a long and arduous journey.
  • 万愁攻人 - Means 'a thousand worries assail one,' expressing deep anxiety or distress.
  • 努力自爱 - Means 'take care of yourself,' a common farewell expression.
  • 后五日 - Means 'five days later.'
  • 潜诣取别 - Means 'to secretly go to bid farewell.' '潜' means secretly, and '诣' means to go to.
  • 其门不复有宅 - Means 'the door no longer had a house,' indicating that the place had changed or disappeared.
  • 乃荒凉之地 - Means 'it became a desolate place,' describing the area as barren and uninhabited.
  • 烟草极目 - Means 'grass and tobacco plants stretch to the horizon,' describing a desolate and empty landscape.
  • 惆怅而返 - Means 'returned with a sense of sadness and longing.'
  • 及京奏事毕 - Means 'after finishing the report in the capital.' '及京' refers to the capital city.
  • 得归私第 - Means 'to return to one's private residence.' '私第' refers to one's own home.
  • 诸赵竞怒曰 - Means 'the various Zhao (family members or associates) angrily said.' '诸赵' likely refers to family members or associates.
  • 女子诚陋拙 - Means 'the woman is indeed ugly and clumsy.' '诚' means indeed, and '陋拙' means ugly and clumsy.
  • 不足以奉事君子 - Means 'is not fit to serve a gentleman.' '奉事' means to serve, and '君子' refers to a noble or virtuous person.
  • 已辱厚礼 - Means 'having already received great respect.' '辱' is a humble term meaning to receive or accept.
  • 亦宜敬之 - Means 'it is also proper to respect her.' '宜' means proper or appropriate.
  • 上以承祖先 - Means 'to honor the ancestors above.' '承' means to inherit or honor.
  • 下以继后事 - Means 'to continue the affairs of the future below.' '继' means to continue.
  • 岂苟而已哉 - Means 'how could it be just a casual matter?' '岂' is a rhetorical particle, and '苟' means casual or hasty.
  • 奈何以妖术致之万里而娱人之视听乎 - Means 'how could one use magic to bring her from afar to entertain people?' '妖术' means magic or sorcery, and '娱人之视听' means to entertain people's sight and hearing.
  • 朱李尚在 - Means 'the red and black (colors) are still there,' possibly referring to signs or symbols.
  • 其筵足徵 - Means 'the banquet is sufficient proof.' '筵' refers to a banquet, and '足徵' means sufficient proof.
  • 何讳乎 - Means 'why hide it?' '讳' means to hide or conceal.
  • 敬伯尽言之 - Means 'Jingbo spoke everything.' '尽言' means to speak everything.
  • 当此之时 - Means 'at that time.'
  • 敬伯亦自不测 - Means 'Jingbo was also uncertain.' '不测' means uncertain or unpredictable.
  • 此盖裴之道成矣 - Means 'this is because Pei's method has succeeded.' '道成' means the method has succeeded.
  • 以此相炫也 - Means 'to show off with this.' '相炫' means to show off.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 吁！ - An exclamation expressing surprise or wonder.
  • 神仙之變化 - Refers to the transformations of immortals or deities.
  • 誠如此乎 - Means 'is it truly like this?'
  • 將幻者鬻術以致惑乎 - A rhetorical question meaning 'is it that the deceiver sells his tricks to cause confusion?'
  • 固非常智之所及 - Means 'it is certainly beyond the reach of ordinary wisdom.'
  • 雀為蛤，雉為蜃 - Refers to the transformation of sparrows into clams and pheasants into sea dragons, a common metaphor in Chinese literature.
  • 人為虎 - Means 'people turn into tigers.'
  • 腐草為萤 - Refers to the transformation of decaying grass into fireflies.
  • 蜣螂為蝉 - Means 'beetles turn into cicadas.'
  • 鲲為鹏 - Refers to the transformation of a giant fish into a giant bird, a famous allusion from the *Zhuangzi*.
  • 万物之變化 - Means 'the transformations of all things.'
  • 書傳之記者 - Refers to what is recorded in books and traditions.
  • 不可以智達 - Means 'cannot be understood by intelligence.'
  • 況耳目之外乎 - Means 'how much more so beyond what we can see and hear?'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 神仙之變化 - A common theme in Chinese mythology and literature, often symbolizing the supernatural and the unpredictable nature of the world.
  • 雀為蛤，雉為蜃，人為虎，腐草為萤，蜣螂為蝉，鲲為鹏 - These transformations are metaphors found in classical Chinese literature, often used to illustrate the fluidity and unpredictability of nature and human affairs.
  • 万物之變化，書傳之記者 - Refers to the idea that many transformations are recorded in ancient texts, emphasizing the importance of tradition and history.
  • 京兆韋氏女 - The Wei family of Jingzhao was a prominent family in ancient China, known for their cultural and social status.
  • 裴爽 - A scholar, likely from a respected family, who is considered a suitable match.
  • 王悟 - Another potential suitor, with a background in military service, indicating a different social and professional background.

Proper Nouns

  • 韋氏 - The Wei family.
  • 裴爽 - A scholar.
  • 王悟 - A man with a military background.
  • 張審約 - Zhang Shenyue, the recorder of the military office and the young woman's uncle.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 又二年 - Indicates a time lapse of two more years.
  • 进士 - A scholar-official who passed the imperial examination, indicating a high social status.
  • 母以告之 - The mother is a significant figure in the story, providing crucial information.
  • 吾之夫乃此人也 - The woman's identity as a wife is central to the narrative.
  • 母许之 - The mother's agreement is a key decision in the story.
  • 遂择吉焉 - Choosing an auspicious date is a traditional practice in Chinese weddings.
  • 既成礼讫 - The completion of the wedding ceremony marks a significant event.
  • 因其母徐问之 - The gentle questioning by the mother adds a personal touch to the narrative.
  • 对曰 - The woman's response is a pivotal moment in the story.
  • 吾此乃梦徵矣 - The dream is a supernatural element that guides the narrative.
  • 然此生之事皆见矣 - The woman's foresight into her future is a key plot point.
  • 岂独适楚金之先知乎 - The woman's knowledge extends beyond her immediate situation.
  • 某既笄 - The coming-of-age ceremony marks a transition in the woman's life.
  • 梦年二十适清河楚金 - The dream sets the stage for the woman's future.
  • 以尚书节制广陵 - Zhang Chujin's high position as a Shangshu (Minister of State) adds historical context.
  • 在镇七年 - The duration of Zhang Chujin's governance provides a timeline.
  • 而楚金伏法 - The execution of Zhang Chujin is a tragic event.
  • 阖门皆死 - The death of the entire family adds a dramatic element.
  • 惟某与新妇一人 - The survival of the woman and the new bride is a turning point.
  • 生入掖庭 - Entry into the imperial harem indicates a change in the woman's status.
  • 蔬食而役者十八年 - The long period of service in the harem adds depth to the narrative.
  • 蒙诏放出 - The imperial decree to release the woman is a significant event.
  • 自午承命 - The timing of the order adds a temporal dimension.
  • 日暮方出宫关 - The late departure from the palace gates adds a sense of urgency.
  • 与新妇渡水 - Crossing the water together is a symbolic act.
  • 迨暗及滩 - The darkness and the shoal add a mysterious and tense atmosphere.
  • 四顾将昏然 - The dusk setting adds a poetic touch.
  • 不知所往 - The uncertainty of their destination adds suspense.
  • 相勉曰 - The encouragement between the woman and the new bride adds a human element.
  • 此不可久立 - The realization that they cannot stay adds a sense of urgency.
  • 宜速渡 - The need to cross quickly adds a sense of action.
  • 遂南行 - The direction of travel adds a geographical context.
  • 及岸数百步 - The distance walked to the shore adds a sense of effort.
  • 有坏坊焉 - The ruined building adds a symbolic element.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 齐纨鲁缟如霜雪 - Metaphor comparing fine silk from Qi and Lu to snow and frost, emphasizing its purity and whiteness.
  • 寥亮高声为子发 - "寥亮" means clear and resonant, and "高声" means loud voice. Together, it describes a clear and loud voice, likely referring to a musical or poetic performance.
  • 嘉宾良会清夜时 - "嘉宾" means distinguished guests, "良会" means a good gathering, and "清夜时" means a clear night. It describes a gathering of distinguished guests during a clear night.
  • 辉煌灯烛我能持 - "辉煌" means brilliant, and "灯烛" means lamps and candles. It describes the ability to hold or manage brilliant lamps and candles.
  • 清冷之泉俟朝汲 - "清冷" means cool and clear, "泉" means spring, "俟" means wait, and "朝汲" means morning drawing. It describes a cool and clear spring waiting to be drawn in the morning.
  • 桑绠相牵常出入 - "桑绠" means mulberry rope, "相牵" means to be connected, and "常出入" means frequently entering and exiting. It describes a mulberry rope frequently being used to draw water from a well.
  • 爨薪贮水常煎熬 - "爨薪" means burning firewood, "贮水" means storing water, and "常煎熬" means frequently boiling. It describes the frequent boiling of water using firewood.
  • 充他口腹我为劳 - "充他口腹" means to fill others' stomachs, and "我为劳" means I am laboring. It describes the act of laboring to fill others' stomachs.
  • 递相褒赏 - "递相" means to reciprocally, and "褒赏" means to praise and reward. It describes mutual praise and reward.
  • 羡其自负 - "羡" means envy, and "自负" means self-pride. It describes envying their self-pride.
  • 虽阮嗣宗《咏怀》亦不能加耳 - "虽" means even if, "阮嗣宗" refers to Ruan Ji, a famous poet, "《咏怀》" is the title of his poem, and "亦不能加耳" means cannot surpass it. It suggests that even Ruan Ji's "Elegies" cannot surpass this.
  • 迟明方归旧所 - "迟明" means late morning, and "方归旧所" means finally return to the old place. It describes returning to the old place late in the morning.
  • 堂中惟有故杵、烛台、水桶、破铛 - "堂中" means in the hall, "惟有" means only have, "故杵" means old pestle, "烛台" means candle holder, "水桶" means water bucket, and "破铛" means broken pot. It describes the only items left in the hall being an old pestle, candle holder, water bucket, and broken pot.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 齐纨鲁缟 - Fine silk produced in Qi and Lu, known for its quality and purity.
  • 阮嗣宗《咏怀》 - Ruan Ji, a famous poet of the Wei-Jin period, and his work "Elegies," which is renowned for its depth and emotional resonance.
  • 郭代公 - Duke Guo, a historical figure, likely a reference to a noble or official.
  • 开元中 - The reign of Emperor Xuanzong of Tang, known for its cultural and economic prosperity.
  • 晋之汾 - A reference to the area around the Fen River in Shanxi province.

  • 《咏怀》 - The title of Ruan Ji's famous poem.
  • 郭代公 - Duke Guo, a historical figure.
  • 开元中 - The reign of Emperor Xuanzong of Tang.
  • 晋之汾 - A reference to the area around the Fen River in Shanxi province.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 公问曰 - Respectful title for an official or gentleman, 'asked, saying.'
  • 堂中泣者，人耶，鬼耶？ - In the hall, the weeping one, is it a human or a ghost?
  • 何陈设如此，无人而独泣？ - Why is it set up like this, no one is here but she is weeping alone.
  • 妾此乡之祠有乌将军者 - In this village's shrine, there is a deity named '乌将军.'
  • 能祸福人 - Can bring misfortune or blessings to people.
  • 每岁求偶于乡人 - Every year, seek a spouse from the villagers.
  • 乡人必择处女之美者而嫁焉 - The villagers must choose the most beautiful unmarried girls and marry them.
  • 妾虽陋拙 - Though I am ugly and clumsy.
  • 父利乡人之五百缗 - Father profits from the villagers' five hundred strings of cash.
  • 潜以应选 - Secretly to meet the selection.
  • 今夕，乡人之女并为游宴者 - Tonight, the villagers' daughters are all participating in a feast.
  • 到是，醉妾此室，共锁而去，以适于将军者也 - When they arrived, they got me drunk in this room, locked me up and left, to take me to the general.
  • 今父母弃之就死 - Now, parents abandon me to die.
  • 而令惴惴哀惧 - And make me fearful and grieving.
  • 君诚人耶，能相救免 - You are truly a human, can save me.
  • 毕身为扫除之妇 - All my life as a cleaning woman.
  • 以奉指使 - To serve commands.
  • 吾忝为大丈夫也 - I, unworthy, am a man of virtue.
  • 必力救之 - Must rescue her with all my might.
  • 如不得，当杀身以徇汝 - If I fail, will kill myself to save you.
  • 终不使汝枉死于淫鬼之手也 - Will never let you die in vain at the hands of a lustful ghost.
  • 女泣少止 - Her weeping stopped a little.
  • 于是坐于西阶上 - Then sat on the western steps.
  • 移其马于堂北 - Moved his horse to the north of the hall.
  • 令一仆侍立于前 - Made a servant stand in front.
  • 若为宾而待之 - As if treating him as a guest.

  • 乌将军 - Deity worshipped in a village shrine, capable of bringing misfortune or blessings.

  • 游宴 - Festive gathering, often involving drinking and entertainment.
  • 淫鬼 - Lustful ghost, often associated with malevolent spirits in Chinese folklore.

  • 乃女之父母兄弟及乡中耆老 - 乃 means 'is' or 'are'; 女之父母兄弟 means 'the girl's parents and siblings'; 乡中耆老 means 'elders of the village'. This phrase identifies the group of people approaching.
  • 相与舁榇而来 - 相与 means 'together' or 'in unison'; 舁 means 'to carry on a pole'; 榇 means 'coffin'. This phrase describes the group carrying the coffin together.
  • 将收其尸以备殡殓 - 将 means 'will' or 'going to'; 收其尸 means 'collect the body'; 以备 means 'to prepare'; 殡殓 means 'funeral rites'. This phrase indicates their intention to perform the funeral rites.
  • 咸惊以问之 - 咸 means 'all' or 'everyone'; 惊 means 'surprised' or 'astonished'; 以问之 means 'to ask about it'. This phrase describes the reaction of the villagers upon seeing the living individuals.
  • 公具告焉 - 公 means 'the gentleman' or 'the man'; 具告 means 'to fully inform'; 焉 is a particle that can mean 'there' or 'about it'. This phrase indicates that the man provided a detailed explanation.
  • 乡老共怒残其神 - 乡老 means 'village elders'; 共 means 'all' or 'together'; 怒 means 'angry'; 残 means 'to harm' or 'to damage'; 其神 means 'their god'. This phrase describes the elders' anger towards the god.
  • 乌将军，此乡镇神 - 乌将军 is a proper name; 此 means 'this'; 镇 means 'village'; 神 means 'god'. This phrase identifies the god as the village deity.
  • 乡人奉之久矣 - 乡人 means 'villagers'; 奉 means 'to worship' or 'to honor'; 之 is a particle that can mean 'it' or 'him'; 久矣 means 'for a long time'. This phrase indicates that the villagers have been worshipping the god for a long time.
  • 岁配以女，才无他虞 - 岁 means 'yearly' or 'annually'; 配 means 'to marry' or 'to provide'; 以女 means 'with a girl'; 才 means 'only' or 'just'; 无他虞 means 'no other worry'. This phrase describes the annual practice of providing a girl to the god to avoid other calamities.
  • 此礼少迟，即风雨雷雹为虐 - 此礼 means 'this ritual'; 少迟 means 'slightly delayed'; 即 means 'if' or 'when'; 风雨雷雹 means 'wind, rain, thunder, and hail'; 为虐 means 'to cause disaster'. This phrase warns that a slight delay in the ritual can lead to natural disasters.
  • 奈何失路之客，而伤我明神，致暴于人 - 奈何 means 'what to do with' or 'how to deal with'; 失路之客 means 'a lost traveler'; 而 is a conjunction that can mean 'but' or 'and'; 伤 means 'to harm'; 我明神 means 'our clear god'; 致暴于人 means 'to bring violence upon people'. This phrase expresses the villagers' frustration with the traveler causing harm to their god and bringing violence upon them.
  • 此乡何负 - 此乡 means 'this village'; 何 means 'what'; 负 means 'debt' or 'wrong'. This phrase asks what the village has done wrong.
  • 当杀公以祭乌将军 - 当 means 'should' or 'must'; 杀 means 'to kill'; 公 means 'the gentleman' or 'the man'; 以 means 'to'; 祭乌将军 means 'to sacrifice to the General of the Crows'. This phrase suggests that the man should be killed as a sacrifice to the god.
  • 不尔，亦缚送本县 - 不尔 means 'if not'; 亦 means 'also' or 'even'; 缚 means 'to bind'; 送 means 'to send'; 本县 means 'our county'. This phrase suggests that if the man is not killed, he should be bound and sent to the county.
  • 挥少年将令执公 - 挥 means 'to wave' or 'to gesture'; 少年 means 'young men'; 将 means 'to command'; 令 means 'to order'; 执公 means 'to seize the gentleman'. This phrase describes the elders gesturing to the young men to seize the man.
  • 公谕之曰 - 公 means 'the gentleman' or 'the man'; 谕 means 'to instruct' or 'to explain'; 之 is a particle that can mean 'them' or 'to them'. This phrase indicates that the man explained something to the villagers.
  • 尔徒老于年，未老于事 - 尔 means 'you'; 徒 means 'only' or 'just'; 老于年 means 'old in age'; 未 means 'not yet' or 'not'; 老于事 means 'experienced in affairs'. This phrase suggests that while the villagers are old in age, they are not experienced in handling such matters.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The sequence of actions must be read as a continuous process: ordering a large group of people to form a circle around themselves with various weapons and tools, and then following the trail of blood.
  • This phrase describes the distance traveled and the location where the blood leads: only twenty li, and the blood leads into a large tomb's cave.
  • This phrase describes the action of surrounding and digging, with the result being a gradual enlargement: surround and dig, immediately becomes larger like the mouth of a jar.
  • This phrase describes the action of lighting a fire and throwing it in to illuminate: the official ordered bundled firewood to be lit and thrown in to illuminate it.
  • This phrase describes the discovery of a large pig inside the tomb, its condition, and its death: inside it is like a large room, a large pig is seen, without a left front hoof, lying on the ground in blood, bursting out of the smoke, died within the enclosure.
  • This phrase describes the reaction of the villagers and their reward for the official: the villagers rejoiced together, gathered money to reward the official.
  • This phrase explains the official's motivation: I am here to eliminate harm for people, not to sell hunting.
  • This phrase explains the woman's background and her innocence: fortunate to be human, entrusted with blood relations, have not yet left the inner chambers, so there is no fault in killing.
  • This phrase describes the woman's decision to marry the beast: greedy for fifty thousand coins, to marry an evil beast, endure the lock and leave, is this suitable for a person?
  • This phrase expresses gratitude to the official: if not for the benevolence and courage of Official Guo, how could I have this day?
  • This phrase expresses the woman's decision to follow the official: I will follow Official Guo, no longer thinking of my old hometown.
  • This phrase describes the woman's decision to marry the beast: many attempts to persuade and stop her were unsuccessful.
  • This phrase describes the woman's decision to marry the beast: eventually took her as a concubine and they had several children.

  • 何得相讥 - Rhetorical question meaning 'How can you mock me?'
  • 向使君得在浑家，一日自当足矣 - Hypothetical statement meaning 'If you had been with your wife, one day would have been enough.'
  • 餐 膳肴馔，引满数十巡 - Describes a lavish feast with multiple rounds of food and drink.
  • 主人至，觅庭俊不见 - Narrative statement meaning 'When the host arrived, he couldn't find Ting Jun.'
  • 庭俊应曰：“唯。” - Formal response meaning 'Ting Jun replied, 'Yes.''
  • 身在厕屋下，傍有大苍蝇、秃帚而已 - Vivid description meaning 'He was in the latrine, with only a large fly and a broom nearby.'
  • 庭俊先有热疾，自此后顿愈，不复更发矣 - Narrative statement meaning 'Ting Jun had previously suffered from a fever, but after this, he was completely cured and the fever never returned.'

  • 忽有二黄衣见顾总曰：“刘君，颇忆畴昔周旋否？” - Narrative statement meaning 'Suddenly, two yellow-clad men saw Gu Zong and said, 'Lord Liu, do you remember our past interactions?''
  • 弊宗乃顾氏，先未曾面清颜，何有周旋之问 - Narrative statement meaning 'My family is the Gu family, and I have never met you before, so why are you asking about our past interactions?'
  • 足下生前是刘桢，为坤明侍中，以纳赂金谪为小吏，公今当不知矣 - Narrative statement meaning 'You were Lord Liu Zhen in life, a Grand Councilor of Kunning, but you were demoted to a minor official for accepting bribes, and you probably don't remember this now.'

  • 总试省览，乃了然明悟，便觉藻思泉涌 - Narrative statement meaning 'Gu Zong tried to read them, and suddenly he understood everything clearly and felt a surge of creative inspiration.'

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 其集人多有本 - Ambiguous phrase; could mean 'most of his works are available' or 'many of his collections are extant.'
  • 卒后数篇记得 - Ambiguous phrase; could mean 'several poems were written by the deceased after his death' or 'several poems were remembered or recalled by others after his death.'
  • 煌煌魏世祖，拯溺静波澜 - Laudatory statement about Cao Cao, but the exact meaning of 'rescuing those in peril and calming the waves' is somewhat metaphorical and could refer to his political or military achievements.
  • 天纪已垂定，邦人亦保完 - Suggests that the heavenly mandate has been established and the people are secure, but the exact meaning of '天纪' (heavenly mandate) is somewhat abstract.
  • 大开相公府，掇拾尽幽兰 - Somewhat metaphorical; 'greatly opening the Chancellor's office' could refer to the establishment of a new government or the appointment of a new chancellor, while 'gathering all the fragrant orchids' could refer to the recruitment of talented scholars.
  • 始从众君子，日侍贤主欢 - Somewhat elliptical; could mean 'At first, I followed the many gentlemen, daily serving the wise ruler with joy,' but the exact meaning depends on the context.
  • 文皇在春宫，烝孝逾问安 - Somewhat metaphorical; '文皇' (cultural emperor) could refer to a ruler who valued culture and learning, while '烝孝逾问安' (his filial piety exceeded the usual inquiries about health) could refer to his deep respect for his parents or ancestors.
  • 监抚多余闲，园圃恣游观 - Somewhat elliptical; could mean 'The supervisor had extra leisure time, the gardens were freely visited,' but the exact meaning depends on the context.
  • 末臣戴簪笔，翊圣从和鸾 - Somewhat metaphorical; '末臣' (lowly minister) could refer to a humble official, while '翊圣从和鸾' (supporting the holy one with harmonious music) could refer to his role in supporting the emperor during imperial processions.
  • 月出行殿凉，珍木清露溥 - Somewhat descriptive; 'walking out of the palace at night, it was cool' and 'precious trees were bathed in clear dew' describe a scene of natural beauty.
  • 天文信辉丽，铿锵振琅玕 - Somewhat metaphorical; 'the celestial phenomena are indeed splendid' and 'resounding like jade and stone' describe a scene of celestial beauty and harmony.
  • 被命仰为和，顾征成所难 - Somewhat elliptical; could mean 'Being commanded to compose a harmonious piece, looking back, it was difficult to complete,' but the exact meaning depends on the context.
  • 弱质不自持，危脆朽萎残 - Somewhat metaphorical; 'my weak nature could not sustain itself' and 'it was fragile, decaying, and withered' describe a state of physical or emotional fragility.
  • 岂意十余年，陵寝梧楸寒 - Somewhat metaphorical; 'who would have thought for over a decade' and 'the tombs were cold, with paulownia and Chinese parasol trees' describe a passage of time and a sense of loss.
  • 今朝坤明国，再顾簪蝉冠 - Somewhat metaphorical; 'today, in the country of K'un-ming' and 'again, wearing the official cap' describe a return to a place of honor or authority.
  • 侍游于离宫，高蹑浮云端 - Somewhat metaphorical; 'serving the emperor in the distant palace' and 'climbing high into the clouds' describe a sense of elevation or transcendence.

  • 人得足食，不假耕种 - Means 'people have enough food, without the need for farming,' indicating self-sufficiency.
  • 原隰滋茂 - Means 'the plains and lowlands are lush and abundant,' describing the fertility of the land.
  • 莸秽不生 - Means 'noxious weeds do not grow,' indicating the absence of harmful plants.
  • 树木枝干间悉生五色丝纩 - Means 'between the branches and trunks of trees, all kinds of colored silk and cotton grow,' describing the growth of colorful fibers.
  • 异锦纤罗 - Describes the fabrics as exotic, fine, and delicate.
  • 不假蚕杼 - Means 'without the need for silkworms or looms,' emphasizing the natural origin of the fabrics.
  • 四时之气，常熙熙和淑 - Means 'the four seasons' air is always warm and pleasant,' describing the climate.
  • 如中国二三月 - Means 'like the air in China during February and March,' comparing the climate to a specific time in China.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 中国 - Refers to China, the central and culturally significant region.
  • 稻粱 - Refers to rice and grains, which are staple foods in Chinese culture.
  • 五谷 - Refers to the five grains, traditionally including rice, wheat, millet, soybeans, and barley.
  • 原隰 - Refers to the natural landscape, with '原' meaning plains and '隰' meaning lowlands.
  • 五色丝纩 - Refers to the five colors of silk and cotton, which are often associated with traditional Chinese textiles.
  • 蚕杼 - Refers to silkworms and looms, which are essential for producing silk.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The sequence '俱发，实色鲜红，映翠叶于香丛之上，纷错满树' describes the simultaneous blooming and fruiting of trees and flowers, the vivid color of the fruit, and the visual effect on the green leaves.
  • The sequence '四时不敢，唯一岁一度暗换花实，更生新嫩，人不知觉' is somewhat ambiguous and could be interpreted as describing a natural cycle where the flowers and fruits change subtly each year without people noticing.
  • The sequence '田畴尽长大瓠，瓠中实以五谷，甘香珍美，非中国稻粱可比' describes the abundance of large gourds filled with high-quality grains.

  • The sequence '原隰滋茂，莸秽不生' describes the fertile and clean land.
  • The sequence '树木枝干间悉生五色丝纩，人得随色收取，任意紡织' describes the growth of colorful fibers and the ease of harvesting and processing them.
  • The clause '异锦纤罗，不假蚕杼' emphasizes the natural origin of the fabrics.
  • The sequence '四时之气，常熙熙和淑，如中国二三月' describes the pleasant climate.
  • The long list '无蚊、虻、蟆、蚁、虱、蜂、蝎、蛇、虺、守宫、娱蚣、蛛蠓之虫，又无枭、鸱、鸦、鹞、鸲、鹆、蝙蝠之属，及无虎、狼、豺、豹、狐狸、蓦驳之兽，又无猫、鼠、猪、犬扰害之类' describes the absence of various pests and animals.

  • 判官曰：“冥司案牍，一一分明。据籍帖追，岂合妄诉！” - Means the judge saying that records in the underworld are clearly detailed, and according to the records, he is summoned, so how can you falsely accuse?
  • 全素曰：“审知年命末尽，今请对验命籍。” - Means Quansu saying if it is true that his years of life are not yet finished, he requests to verify the records now.
  • 乃命取吴郡户籍到，检得吴全素，元和十三年明经出身，其后三年衣食，亦无官禄。 - Means then ordered to bring the household register of Wu Prefecture, found Quansu, who passed the imperial examination in 818 as a scholar, and in the following three years, he had no official salary.
  • 判官曰：“人世三年，才同瞬息，且无荣禄，何必却回！既去即来，徒烦案牍。” - Means the judge saying that three years in the world are like a blink of an eye, and he has no honor or salary, so why return? Once gone, he will come back, and it will only complicate the records.
  • 全素曰：“辞亲五载，得归即荣，何况成名尚余三载，伏乞哀察。” - Means Quansu saying he left his parents for five years, returning would be honorable, not to mention that he still has three more years to achieve fame, and he humbly asks for your pity and consideration.
  • 判官曰：“任归。”仍诫引者曰：“此人命薄，宜令速去。稍以延迟，即突明矣。” - Means the judge saying go ahead, then warned the guide that this person has a short life, should be sent away quickly, and if delayed, dawn will break soon.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 曰：“然則何以言事？”曰： - Direct dialogue format in Classical Chinese, where '曰' means 'said' or 'answered.' The phrase '然則何以言事' can be translated as 'Then how can one discuss matters?'
  • 以吾唾涂人大门，一家睡；涂人中门，门内人睡；涂堂门，满堂人睡。 - Metaphorical expression using the action of spitting on doors to illustrate the effect of spreading information or influence. '涂' means 'to smear' or 'to apply,' and '大门,' '中门,' and '堂门' refer to the outer gate, inner gate, and main hall door, respectively.
  • 可以手承吾唾而涂之。 - One can use one's hand to catch my spittle and apply it, continuing the metaphor of spreading influence.
  • 全素掬手，二吏交唾。 - '掬手' means 'to clench one's hands,' and '交唾' means 'to spit alternately.' This describes the action of two officials spitting into the hands of Quansu.
  • 逡巡掬手以涂堂门。 - '逡巡' means 'to hesitate' or 'to pause,' and '掬手以涂堂门' means 'to clench one's hands and apply it to the main hall door.'
  • 满堂欠伸，促去食器，遂入寝。 - '欠伸' means 'to yawn and stretch,' and '促去食器' means 'to quickly remove the food dishes.' This describes the reaction of the people in the hall.
  • 二吏曰：“君入，去床三尺立言之。慎勿近床，以手摇动，则魇不悟矣。” - '去床三尺' means 'three feet away from the bed,' and '以手摇动' means 'to shake with one's hand.' This is a warning to avoid disturbing the sleeper.
  • 全素依其言言之，其姨惊起，泣谓夫曰：“全素晚来归宿，何忽致死。今者见梦求钱，言有所遗，如何？” - '依其言言之' means 'to speak according to what was said,' and '见梦求钱' means 'to appear in a dream to ask for money.' This describes the aunt's reaction to the dream.
  • 又寝，又梦，惊起而泣，求纸于柜，适有二百幅，乃令遽剪焚之，火绝，则千缗宛在地矣。 - '遽剪焚之' means 'to quickly cut and burn it,' and '火绝' means 'the fire goes out.' This describes the process of burning the paper.
  • 二吏曰：“钱数多，某固不能胜。而君之力，生人之力也。可以尽举，请负以致寄之。” - '生人之力也' means 'the strength of a living person,' and '请负以致寄之' means 'please carry it to deliver it.' This is a request for help in carrying the money.

  • 福不再遇 - Classical phrase meaning 'good fortune does not come twice,' emphasizing the rarity of favorable opportunities.

  • 苟非深分 - Conditional phrase meaning 'unless there is a deep understanding or bond,' suggesting that true insight or connection is necessary.
  • 岂荐自代 - Means 'how could one recommend oneself in place of another,' implying that self-recommendation is inappropriate or presumptuous.
  • 权位既到 - Means 'once power and position have been attained,' referring to the achievement of a high office.
  • 幸勿因循 - Means 'do not be complacent or negligent,' urging vigilance and diligence in maintaining one's position.
  • 生为节制，死岂为民 - Rhetorical question meaning 'If one lives as a general, how can one die as a commoner?' Emphasizes the importance of maintaining one's status even in death.
  • 阳禄方崇 - Means 'official rank and salary are just rising,' indicating the current elevation in one's career.
  • 阴位谁顾 - Means 'who cares about the afterlife?' Suggests a focus on present rewards over future concerns.
  • 直使为王且不愿 - Means 'even if made king, one would not want it,' expressing a preference for a lower position.
  • 上请授公，天命难拒 - Means 'the emperor requests to grant this to you, and heavenly mandate is hard to resist,' indicating a divine or imperial decree.
  • 文符即下，何能违天 - Means 'once the official document is issued, how can one defy heaven?' Reinforces the idea of inevitability.
  • 天听甚卑，亦从人欲 - Means 'heaven listens closely, and also follows human desires,' suggesting that divine will aligns with human wishes.
  • 奈何自取求替，诬其天命乎 - Means 'how can one seek to replace oneself, thus falsely claiming heavenly mandate?' Criticizes self-serving actions under the guise of divine will.
  • 终不受，都统安能与 - Means 'if one ultimately refuses, how can the general grant it?' Highlights the futility of the offer.
  • 必若以鬼相逼，岂无天乎 - Means 'if it's forced by ghosts, isn't there still heaven?' Questions the legitimacy of supernatural coercion.
  • 渴，请两盂茶 - Means 'thirsty, please bring two cups of tea,' a simple request for refreshment.
  • 仆射既不住，不合饮此茶 - Means 'since the chancellor does not stay, it is not appropriate to drink this tea,' indicating a change in circumstances.
  • 况时热，不可久住，宜速命驾 - Means 'especially in the heat, one cannot stay long, it is best to leave quickly,' suggests urgency due to the weather.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 至明 - Means 'until dawn' or 'at dawn', indicating the time when the action takes place.
  • 乃鬻束素以市酒肉 - Means 'then sold a roll of white silk to buy wine and meat', describing the action of selling silk to purchase food and drink.
  • 為待賓之具 - Means 'to prepare for the guest', indicating the purpose of buying the food and drink.
  • 果有五坊猎骑十人来求宿 - Means 'indeed, ten hunters from the Five Fences came to request lodging', describing the arrival of the hunters.
  • 遂厚遇之 - Means 'then treated them generously', indicating the manner in which the hosts treated the hunters.
  • 我猎徒也，宜为衣冠所恶 - Means 'We are hunters, and should be disliked by the literati', expressing the hunters' self-perception and social standing.
  • 今党郎倾盖如此，何以报之 - Means 'Now that Lord Tang has treated us so generously, how can we repay him?', expressing the hunters' gratitude and desire to reciprocate.
  • 因问所须 - Means 'then asked what was needed', indicating the hunters' inquiry about the host's needs.
  • 超元曰 - Means 'Chao Yuan said', introducing the host's response.
  • 亲戚有疾，医藉猎狐，其疾见困，非此不愈 - Means 'My relative is ill, and the doctor needs a hunting fox. His illness is worsening, and it will not improve without this', explaining the host's request.
  • 乃祈于诸人 - Means 'then asked the others', indicating the host's request to the hunters.
  • 幸得而见惠，愿奉五素为酒楼费 - Means 'If you could kindly give it to me, I would be willing to offer five rolls of white silk as payment for the wine', expressing the host's offer in exchange for the fox.
  • 十人许诺而去 - Means 'the ten men agreed and left', indicating the hunters' agreement to the host's request.
  • 南行百余步 - Means 'they walked south for more than a hundred steps', describing the hunters' direction and distance traveled.
  • 有狐突走绕大冢者 - Means 'a fox suddenly ran around a large tomb', describing the appearance of the fox.
  • 作围围之，一箭而毙 - Means 'they surrounded it and shot it with an arrow, killing it instantly', describing the hunters' action to capture the fox.
  • 其徒喜曰 - Means 'his companions were delighted and said', introducing the hunters' reaction.
  • 昨夜党人固求，今日果获 - Means 'Last night, Lord Tang earnestly requested it, and today we have indeed obtained it', expressing the hunters' satisfaction in fulfilling the host's request.
  • 乃持来与超元 - Means 'then brought it to Chao Yuan', indicating the delivery of the fox to the host.
  • 奉之五素 - Means 'gave him five rolls of white silk', describing the exchange of the fox for the silk.
  • 既去，超元洗其血 - Means 'After they left, Chao Yuan washed its blood', describing the host's action after the hunters departed.
  • 卧于寝床，覆以衣衾 - Means 'he laid it on the bed and covered it with a quilt', describing the host's care for the fox.
  • 至夜分人寂 - Means 'by midnight when everyone was quiet', indicating the time when the host took further action.
  • 潜送穴中，以土封之 - Means 'he secretly sent it to a den and sealed it with earth', describing the host's final action to release the fox.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 寓亲腹中 - Literally 'in the womb of the mother,' but it is more idiomatic to say 'in her mother's womb.'
  • 其家以为不祥 - Literally 'his family considered it an omen,' where '不祥' can be translated as 'unlucky' or 'ominous.'
  • 祈神祝佛之法，竭货而为 - Literally 'praying to gods and worshipping Buddha, spending all their wealth,' where '竭货' can be translated as 'spending all their resources.'
  • 擢尚未往，神固何为 - Literally 'Ruo had not yet been born, what could the gods do?' This is a rhetorical question implying that the gods could not help.
  • 兄可往其家，朱书“产”字令吞之 - Literally 'Brother can go to their home, write the character '产' in red ink and make them swallow it,' where '朱书' means 'write in red ink' and '令吞之' means 'make them swallow it.'
  • 擢即生矣 - Literally 'Ruo will be born immediately,' where '即' can be translated as 'immediately' or 'right away.'
  • 必奉兄绢素 - Literally 'they will certainly present the brother with fine silk and white cloth,' where '绢素' refers to fine silk and white cloth, often used as gifts.
  • 兄得且去 - Literally 'the brother should leave,' where '得' can be translated as 'should' or 'must.'
  • 候擢三岁，宜复来视之 - Literally 'wait until Ruo is three years old, and then come to see him again,' where '候' means 'wait' and '宜' means 'should.'
  • 主人孙久不产者，某以朱字吞之，生儿奇惠，今三载矣，思宿以告之，故复来也 - Literally 'the master's grandson who has not been born for a long time, I made them swallow the red character, and the boy is very clever, now he is three years old, I think it is appropriate to tell him, so I came back,' where '主人孙' means 'master's grandson' and '奇惠' means 'very clever.'
  • 可取儿抱卧 - Literally 'can take the child and hold him,' where '抱卧' means 'hold and lay down.'
  • 夜久，伺掌人闭户 - Literally 'at night, when the caretakers have closed the door,' where '伺' means 'wait' and '掌人' means 'caretakers.'
  • 即抱于静处呼曰 - Literally 'then take him to a quiet place and call out,' where '静处' means 'quiet place.'
  • 李擢记我否 - Literally 'Li Ruo, do you remember me?' where '记我否' means 'do you remember me?'
  • 啼即掌之 - Literally 'if he cries, then pat him,' where '掌之' means 'pat him.'
  • 再三问之 - Literally 'ask him repeatedly,' where '再三' means 'repeatedly.'
  • 擢必微悟 - Literally 'Ruo will surely have a slight understanding,' where '微悟' means 'slight understanding.'
  • 兄宜与擢言洛中居处及游宴之地 - Literally 'the brother should talk to Ruo about the places he lives and plays in Luoyang,' where '居处' means 'living place' and '游宴之地' means 'places of play and feasting.'
  • 擢当大悟 - Literally 'Ruo will have a great understanding,' where '大悟' means 'great understanding.'
  • 悟后此生之业无孑遗矣 - Literally 'after understanding, his life's work will be without any remnants,' where '孑遗' means 'remnants.'
  • 此事必醒素以归 - Literally 'this matter must be clearly explained and returned,' where '醒素' means 'clearly explained' and '以归' means 'returned.'
  • 擢乃后荣盛，兄不可复得从容矣 - Literally 'Ruo will later become prosperous and honored, the brother cannot afford to be leisurely anymore,' where '荣盛' means 'prosperous and honored' and '从容' means 'leisurely.'
  • 兄声名籍甚，不久当有大谏之拜，慎勿赴也，赴当非寿 - Literally 'the brother has a great reputation, soon he will be appointed to a high office, be cautious not to accept it, accepting it will not be good for longevity,' where '声名籍甚' means 'great reputation,' '大谏之拜' means 'appointment to a high office,' and '非寿' means 'not good for longevity.'
  • 此郡北三十里有胡村，村前有车门，即擢新身之居也 - Literally 'north of this prefecture, thirty miles away, there is a village called Hu, in front of the village there is a carriage gate, which is where Ruo will live in the future,' where '胡村' means 'Hu village' and '车门' means 'carriage gate.'
  • 言讫，泣拜而去 - Literally 'after finishing speaking, he wept and bowed, then left,' where '泣拜' means 'wept and bowed.'

  • 巫曰 - The witch said, where '曰' is a formal word for 'said'.
  • 将来，吾自收之，暮年服也 - Give it to me, I will keep it for my old age, where '将来' means 'give it to me', '自收之' means 'keep it', and '暮年服也' means 'for my old age'.

  • 长白吾师曰 - My master told me, where '长白' is a respectful way of saying 'my master' and '曰' means 'said'.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 恨为泉台客 - Regret being a guest of the underworld, a reference to death and the afterlife.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 元载 - Yuan Zai, a prominent official during the Tang Dynasty, known for his political influence and eventual downfall.
  • 大历九年春 - The ninth year of the Dali era (774 AD) during the reign of Emperor Daizong of Tang.
  • 建州刺史魏朋 - Wei Peng, a governor of Jianzhou, who later became a guest in Nanchang after his term ended.
  • 建州 - A prefecture in Fujian province during the Tang Dynasty.
  • 南昌 - The modern city of Nanchang, Jiangxi province, historically known as Gan Prefecture.
  • 泉台 - A term for the underworld or the realm of the dead, often used in classical Chinese literature to refer to the afterlife.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The sentence '有献文章者，命左右收之' is elliptical, with '命左右收之' (ordered the attendants to collect it) being implied but not explicitly stated.
  • The sentence '诵毕不见，方知非人耳' is ambiguous about the timing and nature of the disappearance of the person who recited the poem.
  • The sentence '恨为泉台客，复此异乡县' is elliptical, with '复此异乡县' (again in this foreign county) being a metaphorical expression that may be open to interpretation.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 愿言敦畴昔 - Formal expression meaning 'I wish to cherish the past,' where '愿言' is a polite way of expressing a wish, '敦' means to cherish or value, and '畴昔' refers to the past.
  • 勿以弃疵贱 - Means 'do not abandon me because of my flaws or lowly status,' where '勿以' means 'do not because of,' '弃' means to abandon, '疵' means flaws or blemishes, and '贱' means lowly or humble.
  • 诗意如其亡妻以赠朋也 - Literary expression meaning 'the poem is like the late wife's gift to a friend,' where '诗意' refers to the poetic meaning or essence, '如' means like or as, '其亡妻' means her late wife, '以' means to give or present, and '赠朋' means to give as a gift to a friend.
  • 后十余日，朋卒 - Means 'after more than ten days, the friend died,' where '后' means after, '十余日' means more than ten days, '朋' means friend, and '卒' means died.
  • 汝南岑顺字孝伯 - Means 'Cen Shun from Runan, courtesy name Xiao Bo,' where '汝南' is a place name, '岑顺' is the surname and given name, '字' means courtesy name, and '孝伯' is the courtesy name.
  • 少好学有文，老大尤精武略 - Means 'he was fond of learning and had literary talent when young, and especially skilled in military strategy when older,' where '少' means young, '好学' means fond of learning, '有文' means had literary talent, '老大' means when older, and '尤精武略' means especially skilled in military strategy.
  • 旅于陕州，贫无第宅 - Means 'he lived in Shan Prefecture, poor and without a home,' where '旅' means to live or reside, '陕州' is the place name Shan Prefecture, '贫' means poor, and '无第宅' means without a home.
  • 其外族吕氏有山宅，将废之，顺请居焉 - Means 'his cousin, the Lü family, had a mountain house that was about to be abandoned, and Cen Shun requested to live there,' where '其外族' means his cousin, '吕氏' means the Lü family, '有山宅' means had a mountain house, '将废之' means was about to be abandoned, and '顺请居焉' means Cen Shun requested to live there.
  • 天命有常，何所惧耳 - Means 'fate is constant, so what is there to fear,' where '天命' means fate, '有常' means is constant, '何所惧' means what is there to fear, and '耳' is a particle indicating a rhetorical question.
  • 顺常独坐书阁下，虽家人莫得入 - Means 'Cen Shun often sat alone in the study, and even family members could not enter,' where '顺常' means Cen Shun often, '独坐' means sat alone, '书阁下' means in the study, '虽家人' means even family members, and '莫得入' means could not enter.
  • 夜中闻鼓鼙之声，不知所来 - Means 'at night he heard the sound of drums, not knowing where it came from,' where '夜中' means at night, '闻' means heard, '鼓鼙之声' means the sound of drums, and '不知所来' means not knowing where it came from.
  • 及出户，则无闻，而独喜，自负之，以为石勒之祥也 - Means 'when he went out the door, he heard nothing, but he was alone in his joy, believing it to be an omen of Shi Le,' where '及出户' means when he went out the door, '则无闻' means he heard nothing, '而独喜' means but he was alone in his joy, '自负之' means he believed it, and '以为石勒之祥也' means he thought it was an omen of Shi Le.
  • 祝之曰 - Means 'he prayed, saying,' where '祝之曰' means he prayed, saying.
  • 金象将军使我语岑君，军城夜警，有喧诤者，蒙君见嘉，敢不敬命 - Means 'the Golden Elephant General sent me to tell Cen Shun, the city is on alert at night, there are noisy disturbances, I am honored by your favor, how dare I not follow your command,' where '金象将军' is the name of the general, '使我语岑君' means sent me to tell Cen Shun, '军城夜警' means the city is on alert at night, '有喧诤者' means there are noisy disturbances, '蒙君见嘉' means I am honored by your favor, and '敢不敬命' means how dare I not follow your command.
  • 君甚有厚禄，幸自爱也 - Means 'you will have a generous salary, please take care of yourself,' where '君甚有厚禄' means you will have a generous salary, and '幸自爱也' means please take care of yourself.
  • 既负壮志，能猥顾小国乎 - Means 'having great ambitions, can you condescend to visit a small country,' where '既负壮志' means having great ambitions, '能猥顾' means can you condescend to visit, and '小国' means a small country.
  • 今敌国犯垒，侧席委贤，钦味芳声，愿执旌钺 - Means 'now the enemy country is attacking, we are eager to entrust the wise, we admire your good reputation, and wish to hold the banner,' where '今敌国犯垒' means now the enemy country is attacking, '侧席委贤' means we are eager to entrust the wise, '钦味芳声' means we admire your good reputation, and '愿执旌钺' means wish to hold the banner.
  • 顺谢曰 - Means 'Cen Shun replied, saying,' where '顺谢曰' means Cen Shun replied, saying.
  • 将军天质英明，师真以律，猥烦德音，屈顾疵贱 - Means 'General, your natural qualities are brilliant, you are a true teacher, I am honored by your words, and you have condescended to visit me,' where '将军天质英明' means General, your natural qualities are brilliant, '师真以律' means you are a true teacher, '猥烦德音' means I am honored by your words, and '屈顾疵贱' means you have condescended to visit me.
  • 然犬马之志，惟欲用之 - Means 'however, my loyalty is to serve you,' where '然' means however, '犬马之志' means my loyalty, and '惟欲用之' means is to serve you.
  • 使者复命 - Means 'the messenger returned with his message,' where '使者' means messenger, and '复命' means returned with his message.
  • 顺忽然而寤，恍若自失，坐而思梦之征 - Means 'Cen Shun suddenly woke up, feeling as if he had lost himself, sitting and thinking about the meaning of the dream,' where '顺忽然而寤' means Cen Shun suddenly woke up, '恍若自失' means feeling as if he had lost himself, '坐而思' means sitting and thinking, and '梦之征' means the meaning of the dream.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 愿言敦畴昔，勿以弃疵贱 - This line reflects the classical sentiment of cherishing past relationships and not abandoning someone due to flaws or low status.
  • 诗意如其亡妻以赠朋也 - This line suggests that the poem is a gift from a deceased wife to a friend, reflecting the cultural practice of literary gifts and the emotional significance of such gestures.
  • 后十余日，朋卒 - This line indicates a tragic event, the death of a friend, which is a common theme in classical literature.
  • 汝南岑顺字孝伯 - Cen Shun is a historical figure from Runan, and his courtesy name '孝伯' (Xiao Bo) is a traditional Chinese naming convention.
  • 少好学有文，老大尤精武略 - This line describes Cen Shun's multifaceted talents, combining literary and military skills, which was highly valued in classical Chinese society.
  • 旅于陕州，贫无第宅 - This line describes Cen Shun's humble living conditions, reflecting the social and economic realities of the time.
  • 其外族吕氏有山宅，将废之，顺请居焉 - This line describes a practical solution to Cen Shun's living situation, involving a family connection and a shared resource.
  • 天命有常，何所惧耳 - This line reflects the classical belief in the constancy of fate and the acceptance of one's destiny.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 俄然 - Means 'suddenly' or 'all of a sudden,' used to describe the abrupt onset of events.
  • 鼓角四起 - Literally 'drums and horns rise from all sides,' used to describe the sudden and intense sound of military alarms.
  • 声愈振厉 - Means 'the sound grows more resounding and fierce,' emphasizing the increasing intensity of the noise.
  • 順整巾下床，再拜祝之 - Describes the action of 'Shun' tidying his headgear, getting out of bed, and bowing twice to make a wish or prayer.
  • 須臾，戶牖風生，帷帘飛揚 - Means 'in a moment, the wind blows through the doors and windows, and the curtains fly up,' describing the sudden and strong gust of wind.
  • 燈下忽有數百鐵騎，飛驰左右，悉高數寸 - Describes the sudden appearance of hundreds of iron-clad cavalry under the lamp, flying around, all about an inch tall.
  • 被堅執銳，星散遍地 - Means 'wearing armor and holding sharp weapons, scattered like stars across the ground,' describing the appearance and arrangement of the cavalry.
  • 倏閃之間，云陣四合 - Means 'in the blink of an eye, the cloud formations gather from all sides,' describing the rapid and dramatic change in the scene.
  • 順驚駭，定神氣以觀之 - Describes 'Shun' being startled but then calming down to observe the scene.
  • 須臾，有卒赍书云：“将军传檄。” - Means 'in a moment, a soldier arrives with a message saying, 'The general sends a summons.'
  • 地連獯虏，戎馬不息，向数十年 - Means 'the land is connected to the Xiongnu, with continuous warfare for several decades,' describing the long-standing conflict.
  • 将老兵穷，姿霜卧甲，天设劲敌，势不可止 - Means 'old generals and soldiers are exhausted, lying in their armor like frost, facing a powerful enemy that cannot be stopped,' describing the dire situation.
  • 明公养素畜德，进业及时，屡承嘉音，愿托神契 - Means 'Your Excellency has nurtured virtue and pursued your career at the right time, often receiving good news, and I wish to entrust our fate to a divine pact,' expressing admiration and a desire for alliance.
  • 然明公阳官，固当享大禄于圣世，今小国安敢望之 - Means 'However, Your Excellency holds a high office and should enjoy great fortune in this sacred era, so how dare I, a small state, hope for such things,' expressing humility and respect.
  • 缘天那国北山贼合从，克日会战，事图子夜，否灭未期，良用惶骇 - Means 'Because the bandits from the northern mountains of Tianya State have united, they plan to attack at midnight, and the outcome is uncertain, causing great fear,' describing the impending danger.
  • 顺谢之，室中益烛，坐观其变 - Means 'Shun thanked him, lit more candles in the room, and sat to observe the changes,' describing Shun's reaction.
  • 夜半后，鼓角四发 - Means 'After midnight, drums and horns sounded from all sides,' describing the continuation of the military alarms.

  • 潜以改十二年为二十 - Secretly changed twelve years to twenty or quietly altered the twelve years to twenty.
  • 以报存育之恩 - To repay the kindness of nurturing or to express gratitude for being raised.
  • 李逡巡而至 - Li hesitantly arrived or Li came with some delay.
  • 首以图乙改年白李 - First, she presented the petition to change the year to Li or she first showed the petition to Li.
  • 将让之 - Was about to refuse or was on the verge of declining.
  • 万不获一 - Hardly ever succeeds or rarely succeeds.
  • 料必无难之 - Believes it will not be difficult or thinks it will be easy.
  • 欣然谓曰 - Happily said or cheerfully replied.
  • 事则匪易 - The matter is not easy or the task is not simple.
  • 感言请之切 - Moved by the earnest request or touched by the sincere plea.
  • 遂许之 - So he agreed or thus he consented.
  • 临将别 - As they were about to part or just before parting.
  • 请收余骸 - Please collect my remains or please gather my bones.
  • 为瘗埋之 - For burial or for interment.
  • 骸在履信坊街之北墙委粪之中 - The remains are in the northern wall of the street in the Lu Xin Ward, among the refuse or the bones are in the northern wall of the street in the Lu Xin Ward, in the garbage.
  • 夫人既苏 - The lady then revived or the lady then came to.
  • 验而果在 - Checked and found it to be true or verified and confirmed it.
  • 遂以子礼葬之 - So she buried him with the rites of a son or thus she buried him according to the rites for a son.
  • 后申谢于梦寐之间 - Later, she expressed gratitude in her dreams or later, she thanked him in her dreams.
  • 后二十年 - Twenty years later or after twenty years.
  • 夫人乃亡也 - The lady then passed away or the lady then died.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 夫人 - Refers to a lady or noblewoman, possibly a wife or concubine.
  • 冥司 - Refers to the underworld or the realm of the dead, where petitions for favors are made.
  • 李判官 - Refers to Li, a judge or official, possibly a magistrate or a high-ranking official.
  • 履信坊街 - Refers to a specific street in a ward named Lu Xin, indicating a location in a city.
  • 子礼 - Refers to the rites of a son, indicating a high level of respect and honor in burial.

Proper Nouns

  • 李判官 - Li, a judge or official.
  • 花子 - The lady's name or title.
  • 履信坊街 - A specific street in a ward named Lu Xin.

[143]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 狐诵通天经 - Literal meaning 'the fox reciting the *Tongtian Jing*,' where *Tongtian Jing* is a mysterious or esoteric text.
  • 凭棺读书 - Literal meaning 'sitting on a coffin and reading,' which is an unusual and symbolic act.
  • 字不可认识 - Literal meaning 'the characters cannot be recognized,' indicating that the text is difficult or unknown.
  • 足下诚无所用 - Literal meaning 'you truly have no use for it,' a polite way of saying that the text is not useful to the speaker.
  • 愿奉百金赎之 - Literal meaning 'I wish to offer a hundred gold pieces to redeem it,' indicating a high offer for the text.
  • 遂失不见 - Literal meaning 'he then vanished and was no longer seen,' indicating a sudden disappearance.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 裴仲元 - A name of a person, likely a scholar or official from the Tang or Song dynasty.
  • 鄠北 - Refers to a location in the northern part of Huxian (鄠县), a county in Shaanxi province.
  • 通天经 - A mysterious or esoteric text, possibly a reference to a lost or legendary work.
  • 胡秀才 - A scholar of the Hu ethnicity, which could imply foreign or non-Han Chinese origins, reflecting the multicultural context of the time.
  • 韦端士 - A deceased person, likely a scholar or official, whose knowledge is invoked to interpret the text.

Proper Nouns

  • 裴仲元 - A name of a person, likely a scholar or official from the Tang or Song dynasty.
  • 鄠北 - A location in the northern part of Huxian (鄠县), a county in Shaanxi province.
  • 通天经 - A mysterious or esoteric text, possibly a reference to a lost or legendary work.
  • 胡秀才 - A scholar of the Hu ethnicity, which could imply foreign or non-Han Chinese origins.
  • 行周 - The name of the scholar, *Xingzhou*.
  • 韦端士 - The name of the deceased brother-in-law, *Wei Danshi*.
